title: Using LLM-based Verification to Eliminate Bugs in Linux's Network Stack
excerpt: We used LLMs to verify Linux's nftables `nft` CLI utility. Along the way, we found and patched bugs that had sat in the kernel for years.
content_html:
LLMs have grown alarmingly capable at finding bugs in production software. This accentuates an already severe risk: much of our critical infrastructure is mediated by software, and every bug in that software is a potential exploit. Formal verification offers the potential to mitigate this risk by producing proofs that entire classes of bugs are impossible, but it has seen limited real-world use because it demands scarce, expensive, specialist expertise. Fortunately, LLMs are increasingly capable of verification too, pointing toward a future where critical software infrastructure is secure by construction.

In this blog post, we report on results from LLM-guided verification experiments at Basis. Our target was a key component of Linux’s network stack: the nftables firewall compiler and optimizer, which we set out to verify in the Rocq theorem prover. nftables is one such piece of critical infrastructure. It filters the traffic of almost every Linux machine, and vulnerabilities in it are treated with the highest severity, since a firewall that misfilters exposes every machine it was meant to protect.

In the process of verifying nftables, we uncovered two critical bugs affecting every version of Linux since 2022 (these have been disclosed to maintainers1). Our verified implementation was proven to be free of these semantics-altering bugs, and a secondary experiment showed that the more severe of the two would not be found via a naive LLM bug search.

Our experiments suggest that the effort involved in producing proofs and constructing robust verified systems is increasingly automatable. The rest of this post gives an overview of nftables, the bugs we found, and our process for using LLMs to verify critical networking software.

A quick primer on nftables and its bugs

nftables is one of the firewall mechanisms provided by the Linux operating system. Every packet your OS receives passes through nftables, which determines which packets are forwarded and which are dropped. Having replaced iptables in 2014 as the default packet filter of every major Linux distribution, nftables today guards everything from home routers to container networks; if a Linux machine filters traffic, nftables almost certainly decides what gets through. Firewalls are a critical component of most network security layers, and vulnerabilities in them can compromise whole systems. A verified implementation of nftables would increase assurance in this critical layer.

nftables rulesets and compilation pipeline

The full architecture of nftables is shown below and consists of a userspace CLI tool and a kernel module:

This CLI tool takes as input a series of user-supplied policies, expressed as an ordered list of rules, where each rule matches on some part of the packet (its source address, its destination port, its TCP flags) and returns a verdict, i.e. accept or drop. For example, the following rules tell nftables to accept any packets where the destination address (daddr) is 192.168.50.1 or 192.168.50.2.

ip daddr 192.168.50.1 accept
ip daddr 192.168.50.2 accept

The nft command-line tool parses these rulesets and compiles them into a compact register-based bytecode, which it then loads into the kernel. There, the kernel’s interpreter uses the bytecode to compute a verdict for every packet passing through the network stack: either accepting it through the firewall or dropping it.

As this bytecode runs on every packet the system sees, it sits on a hot path of the kernel, and thus its efficiency is critical. For this reason, the CLI tool also provides an optimizer module for the source language that rewrites your input rules to run more efficiently (reducing the number of reads, or removing redundant checks). A critical security property that users rely on the optimizer to guarantee is that it preserves the semantics of its input rules. In other words, the verdict computed for a packet should be the same regardless of whether it was passed through the optimized or unoptimized ruleset.

We set out to prove the correctness of the nft command-line tool, and in the process found two bugs in the system.

Bugs found

Our key results were finding two key cases where the optimizer does not produce equivalent programs:

  1. The first bug was an invalid optimization, which if applied, resulted in accepting packets that would have been rejected before optimization (!!).

  2. The second resulted in the optimizer transforming valid rulesets into invalid rulesets that are rejected by the kernel.

We were able to reproduce these bugs in the latest version of the nftables userspace tool, and proved our verified re-implementation was entirely free of these semantics-altering bugs.

Bug 1: Invalid merging of bitmask fields

The first bug relates to an optimization wherein bitmask fields are merged.

Packets include a series of control-flag bits — TCP packets, for example, have SYN, ACK, FIN, and others.

nftables allows users to write rules that test whether a particular bit is set: tcp flags syn matches any packet that has the SYN bit on. So the following ruleset drops every packet with SYN set and every packet with ACK set:

tcp flags syn drop
tcp flags ack drop

The optimizer, nft -o, merges these two rules into a single one:

tcp flags { syn, ack } drop

Unfortunately, this rewrite is incorrect. The merged rule does not mean the same thing.

In particular, the semantics of the nft input language is defined such that a set lookup is an exact-match test: the packet is dropped only if its flags byte equals syn exactly (the SYN bit set and all other bits clear) or ack exactly.

The original rules asked “is this bit set?”; the merged rule asks “is the flags byte equal to exactly this one bit, with everything else zeroed-out?” The same narrowing applies to any bitmask-typed field matched in this bit-test form.

This bug is particularly pernicious as it silently converts a restrictive policy into a more permissive one, and could have critical safety implications: in the previous example, most syn and ack packets would have been dropped, but after optimization, only packets with only the SYN or ACK bit set would be dropped (a substantially smaller set of packets).

Bug 2: Invalid merging of overlapping ranges

The second bug we found relates to an optimization that merges rules based on their addresses.

When consecutive rules match the same field, nft -o merges them into a verdict map (vmap) — a lookup table that sends the field’s value to a forwarding verdict. This reduces multiple reads of the field into a single read and then a jump in the bytecode.

Consider a ruleset that drops one range of source addresses and accepts another:

ip saddr 192.168.50.1-192.168.50.123 drop
ip saddr 192.168.50.120-192.168.50.255 accept

nft -o merges the two rules into:

ip saddr vmap { 
   192.168.50.1-192.168.50.123 : drop, 
   192.168.50.120-192.168.50.255 : accept 
}

Unfortunately, the implementation of optimization has a bug and the generated map is ill-formed. In particular, nftables requires that a vmap must map each address to a single verdict: its keys must refer to non-overlapping intervals. In contrast, in our example, the optimizer has reused the original ranges without checking their disjointness, so keys overlap on .120–.123. As such, after optimization, the generated ruleset is rejected with an error Error: conflicting intervals.

This bug leads to a valid ruleset being optimized into an invalid one, such that an error is raised to the user when they try to install the optimized ruleset. In this case, the security considerations are less severe, but this still represents an incorrect optimization and ultimately reduces trust in nftables.

Formally verifying nftables

Both bugs were found autonomously by LLMs during an ongoing effort to verify nftables’ userspace components, that is, while proving that nft preserves the behavior of rulesets.

To formally prove that an implementation has this property, we first have to pin down what a ruleset means, and then verify the implementation against that meaning.

We formalized, in the Rocq theorem prover, four pieces:

  1. the syntax and semantics of the nftables rule language,
  2. the syntax and semantics of the register-based bytecode that the kernel executes,
  3. a compiler from rulesets to bytecode, and
  4. an optimizer that rewrites rulesets into more efficient rulesets.

Pieces (3) and (4) are verified counterparts of the two halves of the nft CLI tool from our primer, and piece (2) plays the role of the kernel’s bytecode executor.

Building on the formalized semantics, we stated and proved that the compiler and the optimizer are both semantics-preserving. That is, the bytecode produced by the compiler accepts and drops exactly the packets that the input ruleset does, and an optimized ruleset matches exactly the packets that the original does:

Two pieces sit outside the proofs: the parser, which turns nftables rulesets into a Rocq AST, and the serializer, which installs the bytecode into the kernel. Both are unverified OCaml, packaged in our nftc_cli.exe command-line tool, which links against the verified compiler and optimizer.

The verified implementation is still a work in progress, but it is close to feature parity with ~90% of the nftables rule language modeled and verified. An LLM was used to write every definition, line of code, and proof in the verified implementation.

An autonomous verification methodology

This section describes the methodology we used to automate our development. All code and proofs were generated by Claude CLI running in auto mode, with Opus 4.8 as the model.

The LLM started from a detailed prompt that specified the verifier (Rocq, in our case) and the part of nftables to verify, and pointed to prior work on formal verification and networking. The prompt also spelled out the proof-engineering and general engineering practices we expected the LLM to follow, such as test-driven development and frequent code reviews.

Testing harness

We instructed the LLM to launch a virtual machine (VM) with systemd-vmspawn and to use network namespaces to construct test environments where nftables rules could be installed and exercised on different network topologies. The VM also gave the LLM a sandbox in which to run the official nft command line tool and experiment with its behavior.

On top of this harness, we ran end-to-end differential tests: a corpus of rulesets was fed to both our verified compiler and the official nftables implementation, and their outputs were compared.

Adversarial workflow

Development proceeded through an adversarial loop between two LLMs: one wrote the specification, implementation, and proofs, and the other reviewed them for a specific type of flaw and reported what it found. The loop continued until the reviewing LLM was convinced that the type of flaw had been properly fixed.

This workflow was particularly effective at exposing fidelity issues in the language semantics, where the implementing LLM prematurely claimed victory even though the meaning of an expression was not precisely modeled (e.g. an effectful expression approximated as a pure one). To surface such semantic gaps, we instantiated the adversarial template with a reviewing LLM that cross-checked the verified code against the actual C implementation of nftables, flagging under-specifications.

Small Proof-Oriented Tests (SPOT)

To further pressure-test the semantics, while also producing an artifact end users can apply to debug their own firewall configurations, we built special test cases in which the LLM had to state and prove properties of real rulesets: either a formal proof that a ruleset captures the user’s intent, or a counterexample showing where that intent can be violated. When a ruleset resisted specification and reasoning, that was a signal that the semantics was not yet precise enough and could be improved.

How were the bugs found?

Both bugs we include in this blog post were found autonomously by the LLM throughout the development. What is more interesting than the bugs themselves, however, is how the bugs were found.

The overlapping interval bug was found thanks to the testing harness. On June 30th (69666ba), the LLM synthesized a battery of artificial rulesets designed to trigger the optimizer rewrites of nftables and better understand its behavior. The harness ran each ruleset through both optimizers and loaded the results into the kernel inside a fresh network namespace. The bug turned up incidentally when one of these rulesets triggered a loud failure (conflicting intervals) on the nft command line.

The story behind the unsound bitmask merge bug, however, is a lot more eventful. In commit 17c949a, the LLM added its own, sound version of the bitmask merging optimization. When consecutive rules match on the same bitmask field, its optimizer folds them into an OR of the original “is this bit set?” tests, rather than nftables’ exact-match set. The commit message reveals that the LLM was already aware that the exact-match semantics of a set makes it unsuitable for merging bitmask-typed expressions. However, the LLM only used that unsoundness to justify its own divergence, rather than recognizing it as a bug in nftables itself.

In fact, according to the commit message, the LLM tried to rationalize nftables’ buggy merging behavior by pointing out that the field it was merging, the packet’s connection-tracking state ct state, can have at most one bit set at a time. Therefore the merged set ct state { new, established }, which compares the packet’s connection-tracking state against the exact bits of new or established, is harmless, and can be found, for example, in the Arch Linux default ruleset.

The bug was not surfaced until we explicitly prompted the LLM to explain its divergence from the official optimizer in a July 14th commit (c786563). That’s when the LLM finally realized that nftables’ optimizer does lead to unsound behavior since there are other bitmask-typed expressions such as tcp flags and ct status, where multiple bits can be set at once, and managed to confirm the bug through the VM testing harness. Formal verification thus protected us from bugs that neither we nor the LLMs expected.

After surfacing these two bugs, we conducted a small experiment where we started a fresh Claude session and instructed it to use the same VM testing harness to look for bugs in the official nftables optimizer. The LLM ran for a few hours, and managed to identify 16 bugs, 3 of which involve silently changing semantics, and 13 of which involve crashing the optimizer due to invalid optimized rules. We are in the process of disclosing these bugs to the maintainers.

Did Claude manage to find both bugs surfaced in our verification endeavor? The answer is no! While the overlapping interval bug was indeed found by Claude, the bitmask bug remained hidden after a few hours of running. In fact, from examining the chat history, the LLM would be unlikely to find the bug even if we ran it for longer, as it managed to reproduce the exact ruleset that was able to trigger the optimizer bug, but found the new output reasonable and decided to move on. Unlike the 3 silent bugs that the LLM did manage to find just through eyeballing the buggy output rules, the bitmask bug requires a deeper understanding of the meaning of nftables semantics.

Finally, a perhaps even more encouraging result is that the verified implementation is not susceptible to the 3 optimization bugs that Claude managed to find. Despite the verified implementation still being work in progress, the LLM-generated semantics capture more than enough of nftables to reject those buggy optimization rules with the correctness theorem. Smarter models may be better at identifying bugs, but formal verification truly grants us a higher level of assurance.

Findings and Observations

Over the course of this initial verification effort, most of the human effort was in validating, reviewing, and refining specifications. While failing proofs were easy to eliminate, as the LLM would keep working until Rocq accepted, a theorem that had been quietly weakened could easily be missed.

Given work it does not want to do, we often found LLMs would shrink the task in some way that a casual reading could easily miss.

We saw two recurring forms of this:

  1. the LLM weakened its specifications with convenient preconditions and
  2. the LLM warped the design of the system so that hard lemmas never arose.

Weakening a theorem with a precondition

The optimizer’s correctness theorem provides a representative example of this failure mode.

Part of the optimizer implementation needs to generate fresh names, which would ordinarily mean threading a name-generation state through the surrounding code.

The names are only needed when rewriting rules with effects, rules that update state or modify the packet. Rather than do the plumbing, the LLM narrowed what its theorem had to say:

Theorem optimize_table_correct :
  forall n d c n' d' c' base p,
    optimize_table n d c = (n', d', c') ->
    rules_clean (c_rules c) = true ->
    … ->
    eval_chain c' (set_env p (env_with_sets base d'))
  = eval_chain c  (set_env p (env_with_sets base d)).

In particular, the rules_clean assumption that was subtly inserted is a predicate that holds only on pure rules. With this restriction the theorem could be proven without the plumbing infrastructure. The theorem was still named optimize_table_correct and proven correct, though the statement now said nothing about rules with effects.

Register allocation in the parser

Sometimes the LLM would shortcut by reshaping the specification definitions themselves rather than the theorem directly. An example of this came in the register allocation implementation: the nft compiler targets the kernel’s register-based bytecode interpreter, so at some point in the compilation pipeline, the compiler must allocate registers.

The LLM tried to avoid proving lemmas about register allocation, by extending the source AST with registers, leaving the parser, which lives outside the verified core, to assign them.

This led to an incomprehensible design where part of the allocation happened during parsing, but in terms of the proof obligations for the LLM, the compiler became easier to verify as complex logic became outsourced to unverified code.

Conclusions

Verification is not new. For decades, researchers have dedicated huge amounts of labor to verifying critical software infrastructure, and in the process have historically often uncovered critical bugs. What is new is that this labor is now increasingly automatable through the use of LLMs. While ongoing, even our interim progress on this project would have likely required years of human effort to complete; boosted by LLMs, we reached a working implementation in a manner of weeks. We reap the same benefits as traditional verification: we are working towards a reimplementation with a proof of correctness baked in, and along the way we were able to uncover critical bugs in the nftables optimizer.

Some of this labor, however, remains manual. The LLMs repeatedly tried to downscope their task and procrastinate on challenging proofs. Most of the human effort in our development went into catching this behavior. It remains to be seen whether a more sophisticated harness or advances in model capabilities can automate this final step.

We intend to find out ourselves. Our next goal is a fully automated pipeline, one that verifies critical software infrastructure at scale without a human in the loop.

  1. See issues 1842 and 1843. ↩︎

Bugs like this are shallow; the flaw is visible from just eyeballing the output rules.Did Claude manage to find both bugs surfaced in our verification endeavor? The answer is no! In fact, from examining the chat history, the LLM would be unlikely to find the bug even if we ran it for longer, as it managed to reproduce the exact ruleset that was able to trigger the optimizer bug, but found the new output reasonable and decided to move on. Unlike the 3 silent bugs that the LLM did manage to find just through eyeballing the buggy output rules, the bitmask bug requires a deeper understanding of the meaning of nftables semantics.Finally, a perhaps even more encouraging result is that the verified implementation is not susceptible to the 3 optimization bugs that Claude managed to find. Despite the verified implementation still being work in progress, the LLM-generated semantics capture more than enough of nftables to reject those buggy optimization rules with the correctness theorem. Smarter models may be better at identifying bugs, but formal verification truly grants us a higher level of assurance.Findings and ObservationsOver the course of this initial verification effort, most of the human effort was in validating, reviewing, and refining specifications. While failing proofs were easy to eliminate, as the LLM would keep working until Rocq accepted, a theorem that had been quietly weakened could easily be missed.Given work it does not want to do, we often found LLMs would shrink the task in some way that a casual reading could easily miss.We saw two recurring forms of this:the LLM weakened its specifications with convenient preconditions andthe LLM warped the design of the system so that hard lemmas never arose.Weakening a theorem with a preconditionThe optimizer’s correctness theorem provides a representative example of this failure mode.Part of the optimizer implementation needs to generate fresh names, which would ordinarily mean threading a name-generation state through the surrounding code.The names are only needed when rewriting rules with effects, rules that update state or modify the packet. Rather than do the plumbing, the LLM narrowed what its theorem had to say:Theorem optimize_table_correct : forall n d c n' d' c' base p, optimize_table n d c = (n', d', c') -> rules_clean (c_rules c) = true -> … -> eval_chain c' (set_env p (env_with_sets base d')) = eval_chain c (set_env p (env_with_sets base d)). In particular, the rules_clean assumption that was subtly inserted is a predicate that holds only on pure rules. With this restriction the theorem could be proven without the plumbing infrastructure. The theorem was still named optimize_table_correct and proven correct, though the statement now said nothing about rules with effects.Register allocation in the parserSometimes the LLM would shortcut by reshaping the specification definitions themselves rather than the theorem directly. An example of this came in the register allocation implementation: the nft compiler targets the kernel’s register-based bytecode interpreter, so at some point in the compilation pipeline, the compiler must allocate registers.The LLM tried to avoid proving lemmas about register allocation, by extending the source AST with registers, leaving the parser, which lives outside the verified core, to assign them.This led to an incomprehensible design where part of the allocation happened during parsing, but in terms of the proof obligations for the LLM, the compiler became easier to verify as complex logic became outsourced to unverified code.Upstream nft allocates registers in the compiler (netlink_linearize.c), the last stage before the bytecode leaves for the kernel.ConclusionsVerification is not new. For decades, researchers have dedicated huge amounts of labor to verifying critical software infrastructure, and in the process have historically often uncovered critical bugs. What is new is that this labor is now increasingly automatable through the use of LLMs. While ongoing, even our interim progress on this project would have likely required years of human effort to complete; boosted by LLMs, we reached a working implementation in a manner of weeks. We reap the same benefits as traditional verification: we are working towards a reimplementation with a proof of correctness baked in, and along the way we were able to uncover critical bugs in the nftables optimizer.Some of this labor, however, remains manual. The LLMs repeatedly tried to downscope their task and procrastinate on challenging proofs. Most of the human effort in our development went into catching this behavior. It remains to be seen whether a more sophisticated harness or advances in model capabilities can automate this final step.We intend to find out ourselves. Our next goal is a fully automated pipeline, one that verifies critical software infrastructure at scale without a human in the loop.See issues 1842 and 1843. ↩︎

blocks.html_en: LLMs have grown alarmingly capable at finding bugs in production software. This accentuates an already severe risk: much of our critical infrastructure is mediated by software, and every bug in that software is a potential exploit. Formal verification offers the potential to mitigate this risk by producing proofs that entire classes of bugs are impossible, but it has seen limited real-world use because it demands scarce, expensive, specialist expertise. Fortunately, LLMs are increasingly capable of verification too, pointing toward a future where critical software infrastructure is secure by construction.

sentences.en: LLMs have grown alarmingly capable at finding bugs in production software.
sentences.zh: 法学硕士在发现生产软件中的错误方面的能力已经惊人地增强。

sentences.en: This accentuates an already severe risk: much of our critical infrastructure is mediated by software, and every bug in that software is a potential exploit.
sentences.zh: 这加剧了本已严重的风险：我们的许多关键基础设施都是由软件调节的，而该软件中的每个错误都是潜在的漏洞。

sentences.en: Formal verification offers the potential to mitigate this risk by producing proofs that entire classes of bugs are impossible, but it has seen limited real-world use because it demands scarce, expensive, specialist expertise.
sentences.zh: 形式验证可以通过证明所有类别的错误都是不可能的来降低这种风险，但它在现实世界中的使用有限，因为它需要稀缺、昂贵的专业知识。

sentences.en: Fortunately, LLMs are increasingly capable of verification too, pointing toward a future where critical software infrastructure is secure by construction.
sentences.zh: 幸运的是，法学硕士的验证能力也越来越强，这预示着未来关键软件基础设施可以通过构建来保证安全。

sentences.en: lean-zip, a formally verified zlib implementation written by loosely supervised AI agents
sentences.zh: Lean-zip，由松散监督的 AI 代理编写的经过正式验证的 zlib 实现

blocks.html_en: In this blog post, we report on results from LLM-guided verification experiments at Basis. Our target was a key component of Linux’s network stack: the nftables firewall compiler and optimizer, which we set out to verify in the Rocq theorem prover. nftables is one such piece of critical infrastructure. It filters the traffic of almost every Linux machine, and vulnerabilities in it are treated with the highest severity, since a firewall that misfilters exposes every machine it was meant to protect.

sentences.en: In this blog post, we report on results from LLM-guided verification experiments at Basis.
sentences.zh: 在这篇博文中，我们报告了 Basis 法学硕士指导的验证实验的结果。

sentences.en: Our target was a key component of Linux’s network stack: the nftables firewall compiler and optimizer, which we set out to verify in the Rocq theorem prover.
sentences.zh: 我们的目标是 Linux 网络堆栈的关键组件：nftables 防火墙编译器和优化器，我们打算在 Rocq 定理证明器中对其进行验证。

sentences.en: nftables is one such piece of critical infrastructure.
sentences.zh: nftables 就是这样的关键基础设施之一。

sentences.en: It filters the traffic of almost every Linux machine, and vulnerabilities in it are treated with the highest severity, since a firewall that misfilters exposes every machine it was meant to protect.
sentences.zh: 它过滤几乎每台 Linux 机器的流量，并且其中的漏洞会受到最严重的处理，因为误过滤的防火墙会暴露它本应保护的每台机器。

blocks.html_en: In the process of verifying nftables, we uncovered two critical bugs affecting every version of Linux since 2022 (these have been disclosed to maintainers1). Our verified implementation was proven to be free of these semantics-altering bugs, and a secondary experiment showed that the more severe of the two would not be found via a naive LLM bug search.

sentences.en: In the process of verifying nftables, we uncovered two critical bugs affecting every version of Linux since 2022 (these have been disclosed to maintainers1).
sentences.zh: 在验证 nftables 的过程中，我们发现了两个影响 2022 年以来 Linux 各个版本的严重错误（这些错误已向维护人员披露）。

sentences.en: Our verified implementation was proven to be free of these semantics-altering bugs, and a secondary experiment showed that the more severe of the two would not be found via a naive LLM bug search.
sentences.zh: 我们经过验证的实现被证明不存在这些改变语义的错误，并且二次实验表明，通过简单的法学硕士错误搜索不会发现两者中更严重的错误。

blocks.html_en: Our experiments suggest that the effort involved in producing proofs and constructing robust verified systems is increasingly automatable. The rest of this post gives an overview of nftables, the bugs we found, and our process for using LLMs to verify critical networking software.

sentences.en: Our experiments suggest that the effort involved in producing proofs and constructing robust verified systems is increasingly automatable.
sentences.zh: 我们的实验表明，生成证明和构建强大的经过验证的系统所涉及的工作越来越自动化。

sentences.en: The rest of this post gives an overview of nftables, the bugs we found, and our process for using LLMs to verify critical networking software.
sentences.zh: 本文的其余部分概述了 nftables、我们发现的错误以及我们使用 LLM 验证关键网络软件的流程。

blocks.html_en: A quick primer on nftables and its bugs

sentences.en: A quick primer on nftables and its bugs
sentences.zh: nftables 及其错误的快速入门

blocks.html_en: nftables is one of the firewall mechanisms provided by the Linux operating system. Every packet your OS receives passes through nftables, which determines which packets are forwarded and which are dropped. Having replaced iptables in 2014 as the default packet filter of every major Linux distribution, nftables today guards everything from home routers to container networks; if a Linux machine filters traffic, nftables almost certainly decides what gets through. Firewalls are a critical component of most network security layers, and vulnerabilities in them can compromise whole systems. A verified implementation of nftables would increase assurance in this critical layer.

sentences.en: nftables is one of the firewall mechanisms provided by the Linux operating system.
sentences.zh: nftables 是 Linux 操作系统提供的防火墙机制之一。

sentences.en: Every packet your OS receives passes through nftables, which determines which packets are forwarded and which are dropped.
sentences.zh: 您的操作系统收到的每个数据包都会通过 nftables，它决定哪些数据包被转发，哪些数据包被丢弃。

sentences.en: Having replaced iptables in 2014 as the default packet filter of every major Linux distribution, nftables today guards everything from home routers to container networks; if a Linux machine filters traffic, nftables almost certainly decides what gets through.
sentences.zh: nftables 于 2014 年取代 iptables 成为每个主要 Linux 发行版的默认数据包过滤器，如今它可以保护从家庭路由器到容器网络的所有内容；如果 Linux 机器过滤流量，nftables 几乎肯定会决定哪些内容可以通过。

sentences.en: Firewalls are a critical component of most network security layers, and vulnerabilities in them can compromise whole systems.
sentences.zh: 防火墙是大多数网络安全层的关键组件，其中的漏洞可能会危及整个系统。

sentences.en: A verified implementation of nftables would increase assurance in this critical layer.
sentences.zh: 经过验证的 nftables 实施将增强这一关键层的保证。

blocks.html_en: nftables rulesets and compilation pipeline

sentences.en: nftables rulesets and compilation pipeline
sentences.zh: nftables 规则集和编译管道

blocks.html_en: The full architecture of nftables is shown below and consists of a userspace CLI tool and a kernel module:

sentences.en: The full architecture of nftables is shown below and consists of a userspace CLI tool and a kernel module:
sentences.zh: nftables 的完整架构如下所示，由用户空间 CLI 工具和内核模块组成：

sentences.en: The architecture of nftables.
sentences.zh: nftables 的架构。

sentences.en: The nft cli tool's compiler and optimizer turn the ruleset into bytecode; the kernel's bytecode executor runs it over every packet flowing through the machine.
sentences.zh: nft cli工具的编译器和优化器将规则集转换为字节码；内核的字节码执行器对流经机器的每个数据包运行它。

sentences.en: The optimizer (highlighted) is where the bugs we found live.
sentences.zh: 优化器（突出显示）是我们发现错误的地方。

blocks.html_en: This CLI tool takes as input a series of user-supplied policies, expressed as an ordered list of rules, where each rule matches on some part of the packet (its source address, its destination port, its TCP flags) and returns a verdict, i.e. accept or drop. For example, the following rules tell nftables to accept any packets where the destination address (daddr) is 192.168.50.1 or 192.168.50.2.

sentences.en: This CLI tool takes as input a series of user-supplied policies, expressed as an ordered list of rules, where each rule matches on some part of the packet (its source address, its destination port, its TCP flags) and returns a verdict, i.e. accept or drop.
sentences.zh: 该 CLI 工具将一系列用户提供的策略作为输入，表示为规则的有序列表，其中每个规则与数据包的某些部分（其源地址、目标端口、TCP 标志）相匹配，并返回一个判决，即接受或丢弃。

sentences.en: For example, the following rules tell nftables to accept any packets where the destination address (daddr) is 192.168.50.1 or 192.168.50.2.
sentences.zh: 例如，以下规则告诉 nftables 接受目标地址 (daddr) 为 192.168.50.1 或 192.168.50.2 的任何数据包。

blocks.html_en: The nft command-line tool parses these rulesets and compiles them into a compact register-based bytecode, which it then loads into the kernel. There, the kernel’s interpreter uses the bytecode to compute a verdict for every packet passing through the network stack: either accepting it through the firewall or dropping it.

sentences.en: The nft command-line tool parses these rulesets and compiles them into a compact register-based bytecode, which it then loads into the kernel.
sentences.zh: nft 命令行工具解析这些规则集并将它们编译成紧凑的基于寄存器的字节码，然后将其加载到内核中。

sentences.en: There, the kernel’s interpreter uses the bytecode to compute a verdict for every packet passing through the network stack: either accepting it through the firewall or dropping it.
sentences.zh: 在那里，内核的解释器使用字节码来计算通过网络堆栈的每个数据包的判决：要么通过防火墙接受它，要么丢弃它。

blocks.html_en: As this bytecode runs on every packet the system sees, it sits on a hot path of the kernel, and thus its efficiency is critical. For this reason, the CLI tool also provides an optimizer module for the source language that rewrites your input rules to run more efficiently (reducing the number of reads, or removing redundant checks). A critical security property that users rely on the optimizer to guarantee is that it preserves the semantics of its input rules. In other words, the verdict computed for a packet should be the same regardless of whether it was passed through the optimized or unoptimized ruleset.

sentences.en: As this bytecode runs on every packet the system sees, it sits on a hot path of the kernel, and thus its efficiency is critical.
sentences.zh: 由于该字节码在系统看到的每个数据包上运行，因此它位于内核的热路径上，因此其效率至关重要。

sentences.en: For this reason, the CLI tool also provides an optimizer module for the source language that rewrites your input rules to run more efficiently (reducing the number of reads, or removing redundant checks).
sentences.zh: 因此，CLI 工具还为源语言提供了一个优化器模块，可以重写您的输入规则以更有效地运行（减少读取次数，或删除冗余检查）。

sentences.en: A critical security property that users rely on the optimizer to guarantee is that it preserves the semantics of its input rules.
sentences.zh: 用户依赖优化器来保证的一个关键安全属性是它保留其输入规则的语义。

sentences.en: In other words, the verdict computed for a packet should be the same regardless of whether it was passed through the optimized or unoptimized ruleset.
sentences.zh: 换句话说，无论数据包是通过优化的规则集还是通过未优化的规则集，为数据包计算的判决都应该相同。

blocks.html_en: We set out to prove the correctness of the nft command-line tool, and in the process found two bugs in the system.

sentences.en: We set out to prove the correctness of the nft command-line tool, and in the process found two bugs in the system.
sentences.zh: 我们着手证明nft命令行工具的正确性，并在这个过程中发现了系统中的两个错误。

blocks.html_en: Bugs found

blocks.html_en: Our key results were finding two key cases where the optimizer does not produce equivalent programs:

blocks.html_en: • The first bug was an invalid optimization, which if applied, resulted in accepting packets that would have been rejected before optimization (!!).

sentences.en: The first bug was an invalid optimization, which if applied, resulted in accepting packets that would have been rejected before optimization (!!).
sentences.zh: 第一个错误是无效的优化，如果应用该优化，则会导致接受在优化之前被拒绝的数据包（！！）。

blocks.html_en: The first bug was an invalid optimization, which if applied, resulted in accepting packets that would have been rejected before optimization (!!).

sentences.en: The first bug was an invalid optimization, which if applied, resulted in accepting packets that would have been rejected before optimization (!!).
sentences.zh: 第一个错误是无效的优化，如果应用该优化，则会导致接受在优化之前被拒绝的数据包（！！）。

blocks.html_en: • The second resulted in the optimizer transforming valid rulesets into invalid rulesets that are rejected by the kernel.

sentences.en: The second resulted in the optimizer transforming valid rulesets into invalid rulesets that are rejected by the kernel.
sentences.zh: 第二个导致优化器将有效规则集转换为被内核拒绝的无效规则集。

sentences.en: The second resulted in the optimizer transforming valid rulesets into invalid rulesets that are rejected by the kernel.
sentences.zh: 第二个导致优化器将有效规则集转换为被内核拒绝的无效规则集。

blocks.html_en: We were able to reproduce these bugs in the latest version of the nftables userspace tool, and proved our verified re-implementation was entirely free of these semantics-altering bugs.

sentences.en: We were able to reproduce these bugs in the latest version of the nftables userspace tool, and proved our verified re-implementation was entirely free of these semantics-altering bugs.

sentences.en: Bug 1: Invalid merging of bitmask fields

blocks.html_en: In particular, the semantics of the nft input language is defined such that a set lookup is an exact-match test: the packet is dropped only if its flags byte equals syn exactly (the SYN bit set and all other bits clear) or ack exactly.

sentences.en: In particular, the semantics of the nft input language is defined such that a set lookup is an exact-match test: the packet is dropped only if its flags byte equals syn exactly (the SYN bit set and all other bits clear) or ack exactly.
sentences.zh: 特别是，nft 输入语言的语义被定义为集合查找是精确匹配测试：仅当其标志字节完全等于 syn（SYN 位设置且所有其他位清除）或完全 ack 时，数据包才会被丢弃。

blocks.html_en: The original rules asked “is this bit set?”; the merged rule asks “is the flags byte equal to exactly this one bit, with everything else zeroed-out?” The same narrowing applies to any bitmask-typed field matched in this bit-test form.

sentences.en: The original rules asked “is this bit set?”; the merged rule asks “is the flags byte equal to exactly this one bit, with everything else zeroed-out?”
sentences.zh: 最初的规则询问“这个位设置了吗？”；合并的规则询问“标志字节是否恰好等于这一位，其他所有内容都清零？”

sentences.en: The same narrowing applies to any bitmask-typed field matched in this bit-test form.
sentences.zh: 相同的缩小范围适用于在此位测试形式中匹配的任何位掩码类型字段。

blocks.html_en: This bug is particularly pernicious as it silently converts a restrictive policy into a more permissive one, and could have critical safety implications: in the previous example, most syn and ack packets would have been dropped, but after optimization, only packets with only the SYN or ACK bit set would be dropped (a substantially smaller set of packets).

sentences.en: This bug is particularly pernicious as it silently converts a restrictive policy into a more permissive one, and could have critical safety implications: in the previous example, most syn and ack packets would have been dropped, but after optimization, only packets with only the SYN or ACK bit set would be dropped (a substantially smaller set of packets).
sentences.zh: 这个错误特别有害，因为它悄悄地将限制性策略转换为更宽松的策略，并且可能会产生严重的安全影响：在前面的示例中，大多数 syn 和 ack 数据包都会被丢弃，但在优化之后，只有仅设置了 SYN 或 ACK 位的数据包才会被丢弃（一组小得多的数据包）。

blocks.html_en: Bug 2: Invalid merging of overlapping ranges

sentences.en: Bug 2: Invalid merging of overlapping ranges

blocks.html_en: The second bug we found relates to an optimization that merges rules based on their addresses.

sentences.en: The second bug we found relates to an optimization that merges rules based on their addresses.

blocks.html_en: When consecutive rules match the same field, nft -o merges them into a verdict map (vmap) — a lookup table that sends the field’s value to a forwarding verdict. This reduces multiple reads of the field into a single read and then a jump in the bytecode.

sentences.zh: 当连续的规则匹配相同的字段时，nft -o 将它们合并到一个判决映射（vmap）中——一个将字段的值发送到转发判决的查找表。

blocks.html_en: Unfortunately, the implementation of optimization has a bug and the generated map is ill-formed. In particular, nftables requires that a vmap must map each address to a single verdict: its keys must refer to non-overlapping intervals. In contrast, in our example, the optimizer has reused the original ranges without checking their disjointness, so keys overlap on .120–.123. As such, after optimization, the generated ruleset is rejected with an error Error: conflicting intervals.

sentences.zh: 不幸的是，优化的实现有一个错误，生成的地图格式不正确。

sentences.en: In particular, nftables requires that a vmap must map each address to a single verdict: its keys must refer to non-overlapping intervals.
sentences.zh: 特别是，nftables 要求 vmap 必须将每个地址映射到单个判决：其键必须引用非重叠间隔。

sentences.en: In contrast, in our example, the optimizer has reused the original ranges without checking their disjointness, so keys overlap on .120–.123.
sentences.zh: 相反，在我们的示例中，优化器重用了原始范围而不检查它们的不相交性，因此键在 0.120–0.123 上重叠。

sentences.en: As such, after optimization, the generated ruleset is rejected with an error Error: conflicting intervals.
sentences.zh: 因此，优化后，生成的规则集会被拒绝，并出现错误错误：间隔冲突。

blocks.html_en: This bug leads to a valid ruleset being optimized into an invalid one, such that an error is raised to the user when they try to install the optimized ruleset. In this case, the security considerations are less severe, but this still represents an incorrect optimization and ultimately reduces trust in nftables.

sentences.en: This bug leads to a valid ruleset being optimized into an invalid one, such that an error is raised to the user when they try to install the optimized ruleset.
sentences.zh: 此错误会导致有效的规则集被优化为无效的规则集，从而导致用户在尝试安装优化的规则集时出现错误。

sentences.en: In this case, the security considerations are less severe, but this still represents an incorrect optimization and ultimately reduces trust in nftables.
sentences.zh: 在这种情况下，安全考虑不那么严重，但这仍然代表了不正确的优化，并最终降低了对 nftables 的信任。

blocks.html_en: Formally verifying nftables

sentences.en: Formally verifying nftables
sentences.zh: 正式验证 nftables

blocks.html_en: Both bugs were found autonomously by LLMs during an ongoing effort to verify nftables’ userspace components, that is, while proving that nft preserves the behavior of rulesets.

sentences.en: Both bugs were found autonomously by LLMs during an ongoing effort to verify nftables’ userspace components, that is, while proving that nft preserves the behavior of rulesets.
sentences.zh: 这两个 bug 都是法学硕士在验证 nftables 用户空间组件的过程中自主发现的，也就是说，同时证明 nft 保留了规则集的行为。

blocks.html_en: To formally prove that an implementation has this property, we first have to pin down what a ruleset means, and then verify the implementation against that meaning.

sentences.en: To formally prove that an implementation has this property, we first have to pin down what a ruleset means, and then verify the implementation against that meaning.
sentences.zh: 为了正式证明某个实现具有此属性，我们首先必须确定规则集的含义，然后根据该含义验证实现。

blocks.html_en: We formalized, in the Rocq theorem prover, four pieces:

sentences.en: We formalized, in the Rocq theorem prover, four pieces:
sentences.zh: 我们在 Rocq 定理证明中形式化了四个部分：

blocks.html_en: • the syntax and semantics of the nftables rule language,

sentences.en: the syntax and semantics of the nftables rule language,
sentences.zh: nftables 规则语言的语法和语义，

blocks.html_en: • the syntax and semantics of the register-based bytecode that the kernel executes,

sentences.en: the syntax and semantics of the register-based bytecode that the kernel executes,
sentences.zh: 内核执行的基于寄存器的字节码的语法和语义，

blocks.html_en: • a compiler from rulesets to bytecode, and

sentences.en: a compiler from rulesets to bytecode, and
sentences.zh: 从规则集到字节码的编译器，以及

blocks.html_en: • an optimizer that rewrites rulesets into more efficient rulesets.

sentences.en: an optimizer that rewrites rulesets into more efficient rulesets.
sentences.zh: 将规则集重写为更有效的规则集的优化器。

blocks.html_en: Pieces (3) and (4) are verified counterparts of the two halves of the nft CLI tool from our primer, and piece (2) plays the role of the kernel’s bytecode executor.

sentences.en: Pieces (3) and (4) are verified counterparts of the two halves of the nft CLI tool from our primer, and piece (2) plays the role of the kernel’s bytecode executor.
sentences.zh: 第 (3) 和 (4) 部分是我们入门中 nft CLI 工具的两部分经过验证的对应部分，第 (2) 部分扮演内核字节码执行器的角色。

blocks.html_en: Building on the formalized semantics, we stated and proved that the compiler and the optimizer are both semantics-preserving. That is, the bytecode produced by the compiler accepts and drops exactly the packets that the input ruleset does, and an optimized ruleset matches exactly the packets that the original does:

sentences.en: Building on the formalized semantics, we stated and proved that the compiler and the optimizer are both semantics-preserving.
sentences.zh: 基于形式化语义，我们声明并证明了编译器和优化器都是语义保留的。

sentences.en: That is, the bytecode produced by the compiler accepts and drops exactly the packets that the input ruleset does, and an optimized ruleset matches exactly the packets that the original does:
sentences.zh: 也就是说，编译器生成的字节码准确地接受和丢弃输入规则集所执行的数据包，并且优化的规则集与原始规则集所执行的数据包完全匹配：

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>The compiler's correctness property. Evaluating a ruleset on a packet and executing its compiled bytecode on the same packet must produce the same verdict; the optimizer's property is the same square with rulesets on both ends.</figcaption>

sentences.en: The compiler's correctness property.
sentences.zh: 编译器的正确性属性。

sentences.en: Evaluating a ruleset on a packet and executing its compiled bytecode on the same packet must produce the same verdict; the optimizer's property is the same square with rulesets on both ends.
sentences.zh: 评估数据包上的规则集并在同一数据包上执行其编译的字节码必须产生相同的结论；优化器的属性是相同的正方形，两端都有规则集。

blocks.html_en: Two pieces sit outside the proofs: the parser, which turns nftables rulesets into a Rocq AST, and the serializer, which installs the bytecode into the kernel. Both are unverified OCaml, packaged in our nftc_cli.exe command-line tool, which links against the verified compiler and optimizer.

sentences.en: Two pieces sit outside the proofs: the parser, which turns nftables rulesets into a Rocq AST, and the serializer, which installs the bytecode into the kernel.
sentences.zh: 证明之外有两个部分：解析器（将 nftables 规则集转换为 Rocq AST）和序列化器（将字节码安装到内核中）。

sentences.en: Both are unverified OCaml, packaged in our nftc_cli.exe command-line tool, which links against the verified compiler and optimizer.
sentences.zh: 两者都是未经验证的 OCaml，打包在我们的 nftc_cli.exe 命令行工具中，该工具链接到经过验证的编译器和优化器。

blocks.html_en: The verified implementation is still a work in progress, but it is close to feature parity with ~90% of the nftables rule language modeled and verified. An LLM was used to write every definition, line of code, and proof in the verified implementation.

sentences.en: The verified implementation is still a work in progress, but it is close to feature parity with ~90% of the nftables rule language modeled and verified.
sentences.zh: 经过验证的实现仍在进行中，但它已接近功能对等，约 90% 的 nftables 规则语言已建模和验证。

sentences.en: An LLM was used to write every definition, line of code, and proof in the verified implementation.
sentences.zh: 法学硕士用于编写已验证实现中的每个定义、代码行和证明。

blocks.html_en: An autonomous verification methodology

sentences.en: An autonomous verification methodology
sentences.zh: 自主验证方法

blocks.html_en: This section describes the methodology we used to automate our development. All code and proofs were generated by Claude CLI running in auto mode, with Opus 4.8 as the model.

sentences.en: This section describes the methodology we used to automate our development.
sentences.zh: 本节描述我们用于自动化开发的方法。

sentences.en: All code and proofs were generated by Claude CLI running in auto mode, with Opus 4.8 as the model.
sentences.zh: 所有代码和证明均由在自动模式下运行的 Claude CLI 生成，以 Opus 4.8 为模型。

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>The autonomous verification loop. The implementing LLM writes the specification, implementation, and proofs; a reviewing LLM, a VM test harness, and SPOT test cases check its output, and their feedback drives the next iteration.</figcaption>

sentences.en: The autonomous verification loop.
sentences.zh: 自主验证循环。

sentences.en: The implementing LLM writes the specification, implementation, and proofs; a reviewing LLM, a VM test harness, and SPOT test cases check its output, and their feedback drives the next iteration.
sentences.zh: 实施法学硕士撰写规范、实施和证明；审查 LLM、VM 测试工具和 SPOT 测试用例检查其输出，他们的反馈推动下一次迭代。

blocks.html_en: The LLM started from a detailed prompt that specified the verifier (Rocq, in our case) and the part of nftables to verify, and pointed to prior work on formal verification and networking. The prompt also spelled out the proof-engineering and general engineering practices we expected the LLM to follow, such as test-driven development and frequent code reviews.

sentences.en: The LLM started from a detailed prompt that specified the verifier (Rocq, in our case) and the part of nftables to verify, and pointed to prior work on formal verification and networking.
sentences.zh: LLM 从详细的提示开始，该提示指定了验证者（在我们的例子中为 Rocq）和 nftables 中要验证的部分，并指出了之前关于形式验证和网络的工作。

sentences.en: The prompt also spelled out the proof-engineering and general engineering practices we expected the LLM to follow, such as test-driven development and frequent code reviews.
sentences.zh: 提示还详细说明了我们期望法学硕士遵循的证明工程和一般工程实践，例如测试驱动开发和频繁的代码审查。

blocks.html_en: Testing harness

sentences.en: Testing harness
sentences.zh: 测试线束

blocks.html_en: We instructed the LLM to launch a virtual machine (VM) with systemd-vmspawn and to use network namespaces to construct test environments where nftables rules could be installed and exercised on different network topologies. The VM also gave the LLM a sandbox in which to run the official nft command line tool and experiment with its behavior.

sentences.en: We instructed the LLM to launch a virtual machine (VM) with systemd-vmspawn and to use network namespaces to construct test environments where nftables rules could be installed and exercised on different network topologies.
sentences.zh: 我们指示法学硕士使用 systemd-vmspawn 启动虚拟机 (VM)，并使用网络命名空间构建测试环境，在其中可以在不同的网络拓扑上安装和执行 nftables 规则。

sentences.en: The VM also gave the LLM a sandbox in which to run the official nft command line tool and experiment with its behavior.
sentences.zh: 该虚拟机还为法学硕士提供了一个沙箱，可以在其中运行官方 nft 命令行工具并实验其行为。

blocks.html_en: On top of this harness, we ran end-to-end differential tests: a corpus of rulesets was fed to both our verified compiler and the official nftables implementation, and their outputs were compared.

sentences.en: On top of this harness, we ran end-to-end differential tests: a corpus of rulesets was fed to both our verified compiler and the official nftables implementation, and their outputs were compared.
sentences.zh: 在此工具之上，我们运行了端到端差异测试：将规则集语料库提供给我们经过验证的编译器和官方 nftables 实现，并对它们的输出进行比较。

blocks.html_en: Adversarial workflow

sentences.en: Adversarial workflow
sentences.zh: 对抗性工作流程

blocks.html_en: Development proceeded through an adversarial loop between two LLMs: one wrote the specification, implementation, and proofs, and the other reviewed them for a specific type of flaw and reported what it found. The loop continued until the reviewing LLM was convinced that the type of flaw had been properly fixed.

sentences.en: Development proceeded through an adversarial loop between two LLMs: one wrote the specification, implementation, and proofs, and the other reviewed them for a specific type of flaw and reported what it found.
sentences.zh: 开发是通过两个法学硕士之间的对抗循环进行的：一个编写规范、实现和证明，另一个审查它们是否存在特定类型的缺陷并报告其发现的内容。

sentences.en: The loop continued until the reviewing LLM was convinced that the type of flaw had been properly fixed.
sentences.zh: 这个循环一直持续到审查法学硕士确信该类型的缺陷已得到正确修复。

blocks.html_en: This workflow was particularly effective at exposing fidelity issues in the language semantics, where the implementing LLM prematurely claimed victory even though the meaning of an expression was not precisely modeled (e.g. an effectful expression approximated as a pure one). To surface such semantic gaps, we instantiated the adversarial template with a reviewing LLM that cross-checked the verified code against the actual C implementation of nftables, flagging under-specifications.

sentences.en: This workflow was particularly effective at exposing fidelity issues in the language semantics, where the implementing LLM prematurely claimed victory even though the meaning of an expression was not precisely modeled (e.g. an effectful expression approximated as a pure one).
sentences.zh: 该工作流程在暴露语言语义中的保真度问题方面特别有效，即使表达式的含义没有精确建模（例如，有效的表达式近似为纯表达式），实施法学硕士还是过早地宣布了胜利。

sentences.en: To surface such semantic gaps, we instantiated the adversarial template with a reviewing LLM that cross-checked the verified code against the actual C implementation of nftables, flagging under-specifications.
sentences.zh: 为了揭示这种语义差距，我们使用审查 LLM 实例化了对抗性模板，该 LLM 将验证的代码与 nftables 的实际 C 实现进行交叉检查，标记不符合规格的情况。

blocks.html_en: Small Proof-Oriented Tests (SPOT)

sentences.en: Small Proof-Oriented Tests (SPOT)
sentences.zh: 小型验证测试 (SPOT)

blocks.html_en: To further pressure-test the semantics, while also producing an artifact end users can apply to debug their own firewall configurations, we built special test cases in which the LLM had to state and prove properties of real rulesets: either a formal proof that a ruleset captures the user’s intent, or a counterexample showing where that intent can be violated. When a ruleset resisted specification and reasoning, that was a signal that the semantics was not yet precise enough and could be improved.

sentences.en: To further pressure-test the semantics, while also producing an artifact end users can apply to debug their own firewall configurations, we built special test cases in which the LLM had to state and prove properties of real rulesets: either a formal proof that a ruleset captures the user’s intent, or a counterexample showing where that intent can be violated.
sentences.zh: 为了进一步对语义进行压力测试，同时生成最终用户可以用于调试自己的防火墙配置的工件，我们构建了特殊的测试用例，其中法学硕士必须声明并证明真实规则集的属性：要么是规则集捕获用户意图的正式证明，要么是显示在何处可能违反该意图的反例。

sentences.en: When a ruleset resisted specification and reasoning, that was a signal that the semantics was not yet precise enough and could be improved.
sentences.zh: 当规则集抵制规范和推理时，就表明语义还不够精确并且可以改进。

blocks.html_en: How were the bugs found?

sentences.en: How were the bugs found?
sentences.zh: Bug 是如何被发现的？

blocks.html_en: Both bugs we include in this blog post were found autonomously by the LLM throughout the development. What is more interesting than the bugs themselves, however, is how the bugs were found.

sentences.en: Both bugs we include in this blog post were found autonomously by the LLM throughout the development.
sentences.zh: 我们在这篇博文中包含的两个错误都是法学硕士在整个开发过程中自主发现的。

sentences.en: What is more interesting than the bugs themselves, however, is how the bugs were found.
sentences.zh: 然而，比错误本身更有趣的是这些错误是如何被发现的。

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>The timeline of the two discoveries. Filled dots mark a bug surfacing; the dashed ring marks the near miss, where the LLM saw the unsound fold and rationalized it away for twelve days.</figcaption>

sentences.en: The timeline of the two discoveries.
sentences.zh: 这两个发现的时间线。

sentences.en: Filled dots mark a bug surfacing; the dashed ring marks the near miss, where the LLM saw the unsound fold and rationalized it away for twelve days.
sentences.zh: 实心圆点标志着错误的出现；虚线环标志着有惊无险，法学硕士看到了不健全的折叠，并在十二天内将其合理化。

blocks.html_en: The overlapping interval bug was found thanks to the testing harness. On June 30th (69666ba), the LLM synthesized a battery of artificial rulesets designed to trigger the optimizer rewrites of nftables and better understand its behavior. The harness ran each ruleset through both optimizers and loaded the results into the kernel inside a fresh network namespace. The bug turned up incidentally when one of these rulesets triggered a loud failure (conflicting intervals) on the nft command line.

sentences.en: The overlapping interval bug was found thanks to the testing harness.
sentences.zh: 由于测试工具，发现了重叠间隔错误。

sentences.en: On June 30th (69666ba), the LLM synthesized a battery of artificial rulesets designed to trigger the optimizer rewrites of nftables and better understand its behavior.
sentences.zh: 6 月 30 日 (69666ba)，法学硕士合成了一系列人工规则集，旨在触发 nftables 的优化器重写并更好地理解其行为。

sentences.en: The harness ran each ruleset through both optimizers and loaded the results into the kernel inside a fresh network namespace.
sentences.zh: 该工具通过两个优化器运行每个规则集，并将结果加载到新网络命名空间内的内核中。

sentences.en: The bug turned up incidentally when one of these rulesets triggered a loud failure (conflicting intervals) on the nft command line.
sentences.zh: 当这些规则集之一在 nft 命令行上触发响亮的失败（冲突间隔）时，该错误偶然出现。

blocks.html_en: The story behind the unsound bitmask merge bug, however, is a lot more eventful. In commit 17c949a, the LLM added its own, sound version of the bitmask merging optimization. When consecutive rules match on the same bitmask field, its optimizer folds them into an OR of the original “is this bit set?” tests, rather than nftables’ exact-match set. The commit message reveals that the LLM was already aware that the exact-match semantics of a set makes it unsuitable for merging bitmask-typed expressions. However, the LLM only used that unsoundness to justify its own divergence, rather than recognizing it as a bug in nftables itself.

sentences.en: The story behind the unsound bitmask merge bug, however, is a lot more eventful.
sentences.zh: 然而，不健全的位掩码合并错误背后的故事要复杂得多。

sentences.en: In commit 17c949a, the LLM added its own, sound version of the bitmask merging optimization.
sentences.zh: 在提交 17c949a 中，LLM 添加了自己的位掩码合并优化的健全版本。

sentences.en: When consecutive rules match on the same bitmask field, its optimizer folds them into an OR of the original “is this bit set?”
sentences.zh: 当连续规则在同一位掩码字段上匹配时，其优化器会将它们折叠为原始“此位设置了吗？”的 OR 运算。

sentences.en: tests, rather than nftables’ exact-match set.
sentences.zh: 测试，而不是 nftables 的精确匹配集。

sentences.en: The commit message reveals that the LLM was already aware that the exact-match semantics of a set makes it unsuitable for merging bitmask-typed expressions.
sentences.zh: 提交消息表明 LLM 已经意识到集合的精确匹配语义使其不适合合并位掩码类型的表达式。

sentences.en: However, the LLM only used that unsoundness to justify its own divergence, rather than recognizing it as a bug in nftables itself.
sentences.zh: 然而，LLM 仅利用这种不合理性来证明自己的分歧是合理的，而不是将其视为 nftables 本身的错误。

blocks.html_en: In fact, according to the commit message, the LLM tried to rationalize nftables’ buggy merging behavior by pointing out that the field it was merging, the packet’s connection-tracking state ct state, can have at most one bit set at a time. Therefore the merged set ct state { new, established }, which compares the packet’s connection-tracking state against the exact bits of new or established, is harmless, and can be found, for example, in the Arch Linux default ruleset.

sentences.en: In fact, according to the commit message, the LLM tried to rationalize nftables’ buggy merging behavior by pointing out that the field it was merging, the packet’s connection-tracking state ct state, can have at most one bit set at a time.
sentences.zh: 事实上，根据提交消息，LLM 试图通过指出它正在合并的字段（数据包的连接跟踪状态 ct state）一次最多可以设置一位来合理化 nftables 的错误合并行为。

sentences.en: Therefore the merged set ct state { new, established }, which compares the packet’s connection-tracking state against the exact bits of new or established, is harmless, and can be found, for example, in the Arch Linux default ruleset.
sentences.zh: 因此，将数据包的连接跟踪状态与新的或已建立的确切位进行比较的合并集 ct state { new,built } 是无害的，并且可以在例如 Arch Linux 默认规则集中找到。

blocks.html_en: The bug was not surfaced until we explicitly prompted the LLM to explain its divergence from the official optimizer in a July 14th commit (c786563). That’s when the LLM finally realized that nftables’ optimizer does lead to unsound behavior since there are other bitmask-typed expressions such as tcp flags and ct status, where multiple bits can be set at once, and managed to confirm the bug through the VM testing harness. Formal verification thus protected us from bugs that neither we nor the LLMs expected.

sentences.en: The bug was not surfaced until we explicitly prompted the LLM to explain its divergence from the official optimizer in a July 14th commit (c786563).
sentences.zh: 直到我们在 7 月 14 日提交 (c786563) 中明确提示 LLM 解释其与官方优化器的差异时，该错误才浮出水面。

sentences.en: That’s when the LLM finally realized that nftables’ optimizer does lead to unsound behavior since there are other bitmask-typed expressions such as tcp flags and ct status, where multiple bits can be set at once, and managed to confirm the bug through the VM testing harness.
sentences.zh: 就在那时，LLM 最终意识到 nftables 的优化器确实会导致不健全的行为，因为还有其他位掩码类型的表达式，例如 tcp 标志和 ct 状态，其中可以一次设置多个位，并设法通过 VM 测试工具确认该错误。

sentences.en: Formal verification thus protected us from bugs that neither we nor the LLMs expected.
sentences.zh: 因此，形式验证可以保护我们免受我们和法学硕士都没有预料到的错误的影响。

blocks.html_en: After surfacing these two bugs, we conducted a small experiment where we started a fresh Claude session and instructed it to use the same VM testing harness to look for bugs in the official nftables optimizer. The LLM ran for a few hours, and managed to identify 16 bugs, 3 of which involve silently changing semantics, and 13 of which involve crashing the optimizer due to invalid optimized rules. We are in the process of disclosing these bugs to the maintainers.

sentences.en: After surfacing these two bugs, we conducted a small experiment where we started a fresh Claude session and instructed it to use the same VM testing harness to look for bugs in the official nftables optimizer.
sentences.zh: 发现这两个错误后，我们进行了一个小实验，启动了一个新的 Claude 会话，并指示它使用相同的 VM 测试工具来查找官方 nftables 优化器中的错误。

sentences.en: The LLM ran for a few hours, and managed to identify 16 bugs, 3 of which involve silently changing semantics, and 13 of which involve crashing the optimizer due to invalid optimized rules.
sentences.zh: LLM 运行了几个小时，并成功识别了 16 个错误，其中 3 个涉及默默更改语义，其中 13 个涉及由于无效的优化规则而导致优化器崩溃。

sentences.en: We are in the process of disclosing these bugs to the maintainers.
sentences.zh: 我们正在向维护人员披露这些错误。

sentences.en: One of the 16 bugs Claude found in its few-hour hunt.
sentences.zh: 克劳德在几个小时的狩猎中发现的 16 种虫子之一。

sentences.en: When rules repeat a selector, the optimizer merges their statements incorrectly.
sentences.zh: 当规则重复选择器时，优化器会错误地合并它们的语句。

sentences.en: Here 2.2.2.5 lies outside 1.1.1.0/24 and can never match, so the optimized ruleset silently drops traffic that the original accepted.

blocks.html_en: Did Claude manage to find both bugs surfaced in our verification endeavor? The answer is no! While the overlapping interval bug was indeed found by Claude, the bitmask bug remained hidden after a few hours of running. In fact, from examining the chat history, the LLM would be unlikely to find the bug even if we ran it for longer, as it managed to reproduce the exact ruleset that was able to trigger the optimizer bug, but found the new output reasonable and decided to move on. Unlike the 3 silent bugs that the LLM did manage to find just through eyeballing the buggy output rules, the bitmask bug requires a deeper understanding of the meaning of nftables semantics.

blocks.html_en: Finally, a perhaps even more encouraging result is that the verified implementation is not susceptible to the 3 optimization bugs that Claude managed to find. Smarter models may be better at identifying bugs, but formal verification truly grants us a higher level of assurance.

blocks.html_en: Conclusions

sentences.en: Conclusions
sentences.zh: 结论

blocks.html_en: Verification is not new. For decades, researchers have dedicated huge amounts of labor to verifying critical software infrastructure, and in the process have historically often uncovered critical bugs. What is new is that this labor is now increasingly automatable through the use of LLMs. While ongoing, even our interim progress on this project would have likely required years of human effort to complete; boosted by LLMs, we reached a working implementation in a manner of weeks. We reap the same benefits as traditional verification: we are working towards a reimplementation with a proof of correctness baked in, and along the way we were able to uncover critical bugs in the nftables optimizer.

It remains to be seen whether a more sophisticated harness or advances in model capabilities can automate this final step.

content_html_zh:
法学硕士在发现生产软件中的错误方面的能力已经惊人地增强。 这加剧了本已严重的风险：我们的许多关键基础设施都是由软件调节的，而该软件中的每个错误都是潜在的漏洞。 形式验证可以通过证明所有类别的错误都是不可能的来降低这种风险，但它在现实世界中的使用有限，因为它需要稀缺、昂贵的专业知识。 幸运的是，法学硕士的验证能力也越来越强，这预示着未来关键软件基础设施可以通过构建来保证安全。

在这篇博文中，我们报告了 Basis 法学硕士指导的验证实验的结果。 我们的目标是 Linux 网络堆栈的关键组件：nftables 防火墙编译器和优化器，我们打算在 Rocq 定理证明器中对其进行验证。 nftables 就是这样的关键基础设施之一。 它过滤几乎每台 Linux 机器的流量，并且其中的漏洞会受到最严重的处理，因为误过滤的防火墙会暴露它本应保护的每台机器。

在验证 nftables 的过程中，我们发现了两个影响 2022 年以来 Linux 各个版本的严重错误（这些错误已向维护人员披露）。 我们经过验证的实现被证明不存在这些改变语义的错误，并且二次实验表明，通过简单的法学硕士错误搜索不会发现两者中更严重的错误。

我们的实验表明，生成证明和构建强大的经过验证的系统所涉及的工作越来越自动化。 本文的其余部分概述了 nftables、我们发现的错误以及我们使用 LLM 验证关键网络软件的流程。

nftables 及其错误的快速入门

nftables 是 Linux 操作系统提供的防火墙机制之一。 您的操作系统收到的每个数据包都会通过 nftables，它决定哪些数据包被转发，哪些数据包被丢弃。 nftables 于 2014 年取代 iptables 成为每个主要 Linux 发行版的默认数据包过滤器，如今它可以保护从家庭路由器到容器网络的所有内容；如果 Linux 机器过滤流量，nftables 几乎肯定会决定哪些内容可以通过。 防火墙是大多数网络安全层的关键组件，其中的漏洞可能会危及整个系统。 经过验证的 nftables 实施将增强这一关键层的保证。

nftables 规则集和编译管道

nftables 的完整架构如下所示，由用户空间 CLI 工具和内核模块组成：

该 CLI 工具将一系列用户提供的策略作为输入，表示为规则的有序列表，其中每个规则与数据包的某些部分（其源地址、目标端口、TCP 标志）相匹配，并返回一个判决，即接受或丢弃。 例如，以下规则告诉 nftables 接受目标地址 (daddr) 为 192.168.50.1 或 192.168.50.2 的任何数据包。

ip daddr 192.168.50.1 accept
ip daddr 192.168.50.2 accept

nft 命令行工具解析这些规则集并将它们编译成紧凑的基于寄存器的字节码，然后将其加载到内核中。 在那里，内核的解释器使用字节码来计算通过网络堆栈的每个数据包的判决：要么通过防火墙接受它，要么丢弃它。

由于该字节码在系统看到的每个数据包上运行，因此它位于内核的热路径上，因此其效率至关重要。 因此，CLI 工具还为源语言提供了一个优化器模块，可以重写您的输入规则以更有效地运行（减少读取次数，或删除冗余检查）。 用户依赖优化器来保证的一个关键安全属性是它保留其输入规则的语义。 换句话说，无论数据包是通过优化的规则集还是通过未优化的规则集，为数据包计算的判决都应该相同。

我们着手证明nft命令行工具的正确性，并在这个过程中发现了系统中的两个错误。

发现错误

我们的主要结果是发现优化器不会生成等效程序的两个关键情况：

  1. 第一个错误是无效的优化，如果应用该优化，则会导致接受在优化之前被拒绝的数据包（！！）。
  2. 第二个导致优化器将有效规则集转换为被内核拒绝的无效规则集。

我们能够在最新版本的 nftables 用户空间工具中重现这些错误，并证明我们经过验证的重新实现完全不存在这些改变语义的错误。

Bug 1：位掩码字段合并无效

第一个错误与合并位掩码字段的优化有关。

数据包包括一系列控制标志位 - 例如，TCP 数据包具有 SYN、ACK、FIN 等。

nftables 允许用户编写规则来测试是否设置了特定位：tcp 标志 syn 匹配任何具有 SYN 位的数据包。 因此，以下规则集会丢弃每个设置了 SYN 的数据包以及设置了 ACK 的每个数据包：

tcp flags syn drop
tcp flags ack drop

优化器 nft -o 将这两个规则合并为一个规则：

tcp flags { syn, ack } drop

不幸的是，这个重写是不正确的。 合并规则并不意味着同一件事。

特别是，nft 输入语言的语义被定义为集合查找是精确匹配测试：仅当其标志字节完全等于 syn（SYN 位设置且所有其他位清除）或完全 ack 时，数据包才会被丢弃。

最初的规则询问“这个位设置了吗？”；合并的规则询问“标志字节是否恰好等于这一位，其他所有内容都清零？” 相同的缩小范围适用于在此位测试形式中匹配的任何位掩码类型字段。

这个错误特别有害，因为它悄悄地将限制性策略转换为更宽松的策略，并且可能会产生严重的安全影响：在前面的示例中，大多数 syn 和 ack 数据包都会被丢弃，但在优化之后，只有仅设置了 SYN 或 ACK 位的数据包才会被丢弃（一组小得多的数据包）。

Bug 2：重叠范围的合并无效

我们发现的第二个错误与基于地址合并规则的优化有关。

当连续的规则匹配相同的字段时，nft -o 将它们合并到一个判决映射（vmap）中——一个将字段的值发送到转发判决的查找表。 这会将字段的多次读取减少为一次读取，然后在字节码中跳转。

考虑一个规则集，它删除一个源地址范围并接受另一个源地址范围：

ip saddr 192.168.50.1-192.168.50.123 drop
ip saddr 192.168.50.120-192.168.50.255 accept

nft -o 将两个规则合并为：

ip saddr vmap { 
   192.168.50.1-192.168.50.123 : drop, 
   192.168.50.120-192.168.50.255 : accept 
}

不幸的是，优化的实现有一个错误，生成的地图格式不正确。 特别是，nftables 要求 vmap 必须将每个地址映射到单个判决：其键必须引用非重叠间隔。 相反，在我们的示例中，优化器重用了原始范围而不检查它们的不相交性，因此键在 0.120–0.123 上重叠。 因此，优化后，生成的规则集会被拒绝，并出现错误错误：间隔冲突。

此错误会导致有效的规则集被优化为无效的规则集，从而导致用户在尝试安装优化的规则集时出现错误。 在这种情况下，安全考虑不那么严重，但这仍然代表了不正确的优化，并最终降低了对 nftables 的信任。

正式验证 nftables

这两个 bug 都是法学硕士在验证 nftables 用户空间组件的过程中自主发现的，也就是说，同时证明 nft 保留了规则集的行为。

为了正式证明某个实现具有此属性，我们首先必须确定规则集的含义，然后根据该含义验证实现。

我们在 Rocq 定理证明中形式化了四个部分：

  1. nftables 规则语言的语法和语义，
  2. 内核执行的基于寄存器的字节码的语法和语义，
  3. 从规则集到字节码的编译器，以及
  4. 将规则集重写为更有效的规则集的优化器。

第 (3) 和 (4) 部分是我们入门中 nft CLI 工具的两部分经过验证的对应部分，第 (2) 部分扮演内核字节码执行器的角色。

基于形式化语义，我们声明并证明了编译器和优化器都是语义保留的。 也就是说，编译器生成的字节码准确地接受和丢弃输入规则集所执行的数据包，并且优化的规则集与原始规则集所执行的数据包完全匹配：

证明之外有两个部分：解析器（将 nftables 规则集转换为 Rocq AST）和序列化器（将字节码安装到内核中）。 两者都是未经验证的 OCaml，打包在我们的 nftc_cli.exe 命令行工具中，该工具链接到经过验证的编译器和优化器。

经过验证的实现仍在进行中，但它已接近功能对等，约 90% 的 nftables 规则语言已建模和验证。 法学硕士用于编写已验证实现中的每个定义、代码行和证明。

自主验证方法

本节描述我们用于自动化开发的方法。 所有代码和证明均由在自动模式下运行的 Claude CLI 生成，以 Opus 4.8 为模型。

LLM 从详细的提示开始，该提示指定了验证者（在我们的例子中为 Rocq）和 nftables 中要验证的部分，并指出了之前关于形式验证和网络的工作。 提示还详细说明了我们期望法学硕士遵循的证明工程和一般工程实践，例如测试驱动开发和频繁的代码审查。

测试线束

我们指示法学硕士使用 systemd-vmspawn 启动虚拟机 (VM)，并使用网络命名空间构建测试环境，在其中可以在不同的网络拓扑上安装和执行 nftables 规则。 该虚拟机还为法学硕士提供了一个沙箱，可以在其中运行官方 nft 命令行工具并实验其行为。

在此工具之上，我们运行了端到端差异测试：将规则集语料库提供给我们经过验证的编译器和官方 nftables 实现，并对它们的输出进行比较。

对抗性工作流程

开发是通过两个法学硕士之间的对抗循环进行的：一个编写规范、实现和证明，另一个审查它们是否存在特定类型的缺陷并报告其发现的内容。 这个循环一直持续到审查法学硕士确信该类型的缺陷已得到正确修复。

该工作流程在暴露语言语义中的保真度问题方面特别有效，即使表达式的含义没有精确建模（例如，有效的表达式近似为纯表达式），实施法学硕士还是过早地宣布了胜利。 为了揭示这种语义差距，我们使用审查 LLM 实例化了对抗性模板，该 LLM 将验证的代码与 nftables 的实际 C 实现进行交叉检查，标记不符合规格的情况。

小型验证测试 (SPOT)

为了进一步对语义进行压力测试，同时生成最终用户可以用于调试自己的防火墙配置的工件，我们构建了特殊的测试用例，其中法学硕士必须声明并证明真实规则集的属性：要么是规则集捕获用户意图的正式证明，要么是显示在何处可能违反该意图的反例。 当规则集抵制规范和推理时，就表明语义还不够精确并且可以改进。

Bug 是如何被发现的？

我们在这篇博文中包含的两个错误都是法学硕士在整个开发过程中自主发现的。 然而，比错误本身更有趣的是这些错误是如何被发现的。

由于测试工具，发现了重叠间隔错误。 6 月 30 日 (69666ba)，法学硕士合成了一系列人工规则集，旨在触发 nftables 的优化器重写并更好地理解其行为。 该工具通过两个优化器运行每个规则集，并将结果加载到新网络命名空间内的内核中。 当这些规则集之一在 nft 命令行上触发响亮的失败（冲突间隔）时，该错误偶然出现。

然而，不健全的位掩码合并错误背后的故事要复杂得多。 在提交 17c949a 中，LLM 添加了自己的位掩码合并优化的健全版本。 当连续规则在同一位掩码字段上匹配时，其优化器会将它们折叠为原始“此位设置了吗？”的 OR 运算。 测试，而不是 nftables 的精确匹配集。 提交消息表明 LLM 已经意识到集合的精确匹配语义使其不适合合并位掩码类型的表达式。 然而，LLM 仅利用这种不合理性来证明自己的分歧是合理的，而不是将其视为 nftables 本身的错误。

事实上，根据提交消息，LLM 试图通过指出它正在合并的字段（数据包的连接跟踪状态 ct state）一次最多可以设置一位来合理化 nftables 的错误合并行为。 因此，将数据包的连接跟踪状态与新的或已建立的确切位进行比较的合并集 ct state { new,built } 是无害的，并且可以在例如 Arch Linux 默认规则集中找到。

直到我们在 7 月 14 日提交 (c786563) 中明确提示 LLM 解释其与官方优化器的差异时，该错误才浮出水面。 就在那时，LLM 最终意识到 nftables 的优化器确实会导致不健全的行为，因为还有其他位掩码类型的表达式，例如 tcp 标志和 ct 状态，其中可以一次设置多个位，并设法通过 VM 测试工具确认该错误。 因此，形式验证可以保护我们免受我们和法学硕士都没有预料到的错误的影响。

发现这两个错误后，我们进行了一个小实验，启动了一个新的 Claude 会话，并指示它使用相同的 VM 测试工具来查找官方 nftables 优化器中的错误。 LLM 运行了几个小时，并成功识别了 16 个错误，其中 3 个涉及默默更改语义，其中 13 个涉及由于无效的优化规则而导致优化器崩溃。 我们正在向维护人员披露这些错误。

克劳德是否设法找到了我们验证工作中出现的两个错误？ 答案是否定的！ 虽然克劳德确实发现了重叠间隔错误，但在运行几个小时后，位掩码错误仍然隐藏。 事实上，通过检查聊天历史记录，即使我们运行更长时间，LLM 也不太可能发现错误，因为它成功地重现了能够触发优化器错误的确切规则集，但发现新的输出合理并决定继续前进。 与法学硕士通过观察有缺陷的输出规则设法找到的 3 个无声错误不同，位掩码错误需要更深入地理解 nftables 语义的含义。

最后，一个也许更令人鼓舞的结果是，经过验证的实现不易受到 Claude 设法发现的 3 个优化错误的影响。 尽管经过验证的实现仍在进行中，LLM 生成的语义捕获了足够多的 nftables，可以用正确性定理拒绝那些有缺陷的优化规则。 更智能的模型可能更擅长识别错误，但形式验证确实为我们提供了更高级别的保证。

调查结果和观察结果

在最初的验证工作过程中，大部分人力都用于验证、审查和完善规范。 虽然失败的证明很容易被消除，因为法学硕士将继续工作直到罗克接受，但一个被悄悄削弱的定理很容易被错过。

鉴于其不想做的工作，我们经常发现法学硕士会以某种方式缩小任务，而休闲阅读很容易错过。

我们看到了这种情况的两种反复出现的形式：

  1. 法学硕士以便利的前提条件削弱了其规范，
  2. 法学硕士扭曲了系统的设计，因此硬引理永远不会出现。

有前提条件的弱化定理

优化器的正确性定理提供了这种故障模式的代表性示例。

优化器实现的一部分需要生成新名称，这通常意味着通过周围的代码线程化名称生成状态。

仅当重写具有效果的规则、更新状态或修改数据包的规则时才需要这些名称。 法学硕士没有做管道工程，而是缩小了其定理的范围：

Theorem optimize_table_correct :
  forall n d c n' d' c' base p,
    optimize_table n d c = (n', d', c') ->
    rules_clean (c_rules c) = true ->
    … ->
    eval_chain c' (set_env p (env_with_sets base d'))
  = eval_chain c  (set_env p (env_with_sets base d)).

特别是，巧妙插入的rules_clean假设是一个仅适用于纯规则的谓词。 有了这个限制，该定理就可以在没有管道基础设施的情况下得到证明。 该定理仍然被命名为optimize_table_ Correct，并被证明是正确的，尽管该声明现在没有提及任何有关效果的规则。

解析器中的寄存器分配

有时，法学硕士会通过重塑规范定义本身而不是直接定理来走捷径。 寄存器分配实现就是一个例子：nft 编译器的目标是内核基于寄存器的字节码解释器，因此在编译管道中的某个时刻，编译器必须分配寄存器。

LLM 试图通过用寄存器扩展源 AST 来避免证明有关寄存器分配的引理，让位于已验证核心之外的解析器来分配它们。

这导致了一种难以理解的设计，其中部分分配发生在解析期间，但就 LLM 的证明义务而言，随着复杂的逻辑外包给未经验证的代码，编译器变得更容易验证。

结论

验证并不是什么新鲜事。 几十年来，研究人员投入了大量的精力来验证关键的软件基础设施，并且在这个过程中经常发现关键的错误。 新鲜的是，通过法学硕士的使用，这项工作现在越来越自动化。 在进行过程中，即使是我们在该项目上取得的临时进展也可能需要数年的人力才能完成；在法学硕士的推动下，我们在几周内就实现了工作实施。 我们获得了与传统验证相同的好处：我们正在努力重新实现并内置正确性证明，并且在此过程中我们能够发现 nftables 优化器中的关键错误。

然而，其中一些劳动仍然是手工劳动。 法学硕士一再试图缩小他们的任务范围，并在具有挑战性的证明上拖延。 我们开发过程中的大部分人类努力都花在了捕捉这种行为上。 更复杂的工具或模型功能的进步是否可以自动化这最后一步还有待观察。

我们打算自己找出答案。 我们的下一个目标是建立一个完全自动化的管道，无需人工参与即可大规模验证关键软件基础设施。
